1. The width of the golden rectangle is given as 2 meters. The golden ratio φ is defined as φ = (1 + √5) / 2 ≈ 1.618. The length of the rectangle can be calculated using the property of the golden ratio, where the ratio of the length to the width is φ. Therefore, the length is:

$$
\text{Length} = \phi \times \text{Width} = \frac{1 + \sqrt{5}}{2} \times 2 = 1 + \sqrt{5}
$$

The area of the rectangle is:

$$
\text{Area} = \text{Length} \times \text{Width} = (1 + \sqrt{5}) \times 2 = 2 + 2\sqrt{5}
$$

The diagonal of the rectangle can be found using the Pythagorean theorem:

$$
\text{Diagonal} = \sqrt{\text{Length}^2 + \text{Width}^2} = \sqrt{(1 + \sqrt{5})^2 + 2^2}
$$

Expanding the square:

$$
(1 + \sqrt{5})^2 = 1 + 2\sqrt{5} + 5 = 6 + 2\sqrt{5}
$$

$$
\text{Diagonal} = \sqrt{6 + 2\sqrt{5} + 4} = \sqrt{10 + 2\sqrt{5}}
$$

2. The circle is inscribed within the golden rectangle, meaning it is tangent to all four sides of the rectangle. The diameter of the circle is equal to the width of the rectangle, which is 2 meters. Therefore, the radius of the inscribed circle is:

$$
\text{Radius} = \frac{\text{Diameter}}{2} = \frac{2}{2} = 1 \text{ meter}
$$

The area of the region inside the rectangle but outside the circle is the area of the rectangle minus the area of the circle:

$$
\text{Area of Region} = \text{Area of Rectangle} - \text{Area of Circle}
$$

$$
\text{Area of Circle} = \pi \times \text{Radius}^2 = \pi \times 1^2 = \pi
$$
